title: Fulbright U.S. Student Program: Open Study/Research Award - New Zealand
description: The Fulbright U.S. Student Program funds American citizens to pursue independent postgraduate study or research at a New Zealand university or research institution for up to one academic year. Grantees arrive in New Zealand in late January/early February to align with the NZ academic year and receive an in-country orientation from Fulbright New Zealand. The award is open to any academic field and is administered by the U.S. Department of State via the Institute of International Education.
org: Fulbright U.S. Student Program / Fulbright New Zealand
visaNote: Fulbright New Zealand assists grantees with the required New Zealand student/research visa as part of the award orientation process.

title: Fulbright U.S. Student Program: Open Study/Research Award - Uruguay
description: The Fulbright U.S. Student Program funds American citizens to conduct independent graduate-level study or research in Uruguay, with grants running from March to December. Applicants design their own research or study project and affiliate with a Uruguayan university or institution for academic support during the grant. Advanced doctoral students are preferred, but graduating seniors, master's students, and recent graduates with strong proposals are also considered.
org: Fulbright U.S. Student Program / Comisión Fulbright Uruguay
visaNote: Comisión Fulbright Uruguay assists grantees with the residence/study permit required to carry out the grant in Uruguay.

title: Fulbright U.S. Student Program: Open Study/Research Award - Costa Rica
description: The Fulbright U.S. Student Program funds American citizens to pursue independent postgraduate study or research in Costa Rica, with grants typically starting between August and March. Grantees design their own study or research project and establish an academic affiliation with a Costa Rican university or institute for support during the grant. The award is open to any field of study and is administered by the U.S. Department of State via the Institute of International Education.
org: Fulbright U.S. Student Program / U.S. Embassy Costa Rica
visaNote: The Fulbright Commission assists grantees with the residence/study permit required to carry out the grant in Costa Rica.

title: Peace Corps Volunteer - Paraguay
description: Peace Corps Volunteers serve two years in Paraguay on community-identified projects in Community Economic Development, Environment, or Health, following 10 weeks of in-country pre-service training in Spanish and Guarani. Paraguay is one of Peace Corps' largest current posts, with about 100 new volunteers arriving each year. Peace Corps covers transportation to and from Paraguay, housing, a monthly living allowance, and medical/dental care, plus a readjustment allowance paid at completion of service.
visaNote: Peace Corps arranges the diplomatic status/visa needed for volunteers to live and serve in Paraguay; no separate visa application is required from applicants.

title: Peace Corps Volunteer - Guatemala
description: Peace Corps Volunteers serve two years in Guatemala's Western Highlands on Youth in Development, Maternal and Child Health, or Rural Agricultural Extension projects, working through Guatemalan ministries and NGOs. Volunteers complete 10 weeks of pre-service training in Santa Lucía Milpas Altas before being placed in host communities across six departments. Peace Corps covers transportation, housing, a monthly living allowance, and medical care, plus a readjustment allowance paid at completion of service.
visaNote: Peace Corps arranges the diplomatic status/visa needed for volunteers to live and serve in Guatemala; no separate visa application is required from applicants.

title: Peace Corps Volunteer - Dominican Republic
description: Peace Corps Volunteers serve two years in the Dominican Republic on Childhood Literacy or Youth and Community Economic Development projects, partnering with local schools and community organizations. Volunteers complete pre-service training in Spanish before beginning their two-year community placement. Peace Corps covers transportation, housing, a monthly living allowance, and medical/dental care, plus a readjustment allowance paid at completion of service.
visaNote: Peace Corps arranges the diplomatic status/visa needed for volunteers to live and serve in the Dominican Republic; no separate visa application is required from applicants.

title: Peace Corps Volunteer - Jamaica
description: Peace Corps Volunteers serve two years in rural Jamaica on the Primary Literacy Project or the Sustainable Agriculture and Livelihoods Initiative, living with host families in the island's interior and mountainous regions. Volunteers work directly with Jamaican government ministries on community-identified education and agriculture priorities. Peace Corps covers transportation, housing, a monthly living allowance, and medical care, plus a readjustment allowance paid at completion of service.
visaNote: Peace Corps arranges the diplomatic status/visa needed for volunteers to live and serve in Jamaica; no separate visa application is required from applicants.

title: Peace Corps Volunteer - Samoa
description: Peace Corps Volunteers serve two years in Samoa on the Primary School English Literacy Project or the Ridge-to-Reef Resilient Samoa environmental program, partnering with Samoa's Ministry of Education or Ministry of Natural Resources and Environment. Volunteers learn Samoan during training and live in local communities for the duration of service. Peace Corps covers transportation, housing, a monthly living allowance, and medical care, plus a readjustment allowance paid at completion of service.
visaNote: Peace Corps arranges the diplomatic status/visa needed for volunteers to live and serve in Samoa; no separate visa application is required from applicants.

title: Australian Volunteers Program - Papua New Guinea Assignments
description: The Australian Volunteers Program, funded by the Australian Government (DFAT), places skilled Australian professionals into in-country assignments with partner organizations across Papua New Guinea, typically for 3-12 months. The program covers international flights, insurance, and a monthly living and accommodation allowance, plus a settling-in allowance and a resettlement allowance at the end of the assignment. Assignments span sectors such as health, education, governance, and communications and are posted on a rolling basis.
visaNote: The Australian Volunteers Program arranges the visa/work permit required for volunteers on assignment in Papua New Guinea.
